product.name: Initializer
product.summary: A PRD-driven project initializer that takes a textual project description and CLI answers, detects a product archetype, derives stack/features/capabilities, synthesizes architecture and implementation stories, and writes structured project artifacts.
product.iteration_goal: Understand what the project currently does, identify where implementation diverges from intended behavior, and prepare a safe path for a later implementation iteration.

main_idea: The system should accept a high-level project description from the user.
The system should classify the project into an archetype.
The system should derive stack, features, and capabilities from that archetype and user answers.
The system should generate structured artifacts such as PRD, architecture documentation, and implementation stories.
The system should enrich, validate, and refine the generated project definition through specialized engines.
non_goals_for_this_iteration: Do not redesign the repository into a different product.
Do not turn the repository into a production CMS or production application.
Do not remove the engine-based design just to simplify implementation.
Do not merge archetype, capability, and feature into a single undifferentiated concept.

required_outcome: Learn current repository behavior from implementation.
Compare current behavior against intended behavior.
Prepare implementation guardrails for the next iteration.

problem_statement: The repository appears to have a coherent core idea, but current implementation likely contains mismatched contracts, inconsistent naming, partial or missing canonical fields, and pipeline stages that may discard or ignore earlier enrichments. These issues make the generator brittle and risky to refactor without structured diagnosis.

personas.name: Product Engineer
personas.goal: Generate a structured starting point for a new software project from a textual prompt.

personas.name: Technical Lead
personas.goal: Use generated artifacts to align implementation planning and architecture.

personas.name: Execution Agent
personas.goal: Read repository artifacts and safely continue diagnosis or implementation work.

what_the_agent_must_learn: What the CLI currently asks for.
How the archetype is currently detected.
What fields are produced by archetype detection.
How capabilities are currently represented or inferred.
Which downstream engines consume the generated spec.
What files are written to disk.
Where documented intent and implemented behavior diverge.

archetype.definition: A product-type classification that determines default stack, features, and possibly capabilities.

capability.definition: An architectural or platform-level enrichment applied to the generated project model.

feature.definition: A user-facing or product-facing functionality expected in the generated output.

answers.definition: Interactive CLI inputs selected by the user and used to shape generated output.

outputs.definition: Artifacts written by the initializer after synthesis.

required_engines.name: archetype_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Detect archetype and return canonical archetype metadata.

required_engines.name: capability_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Apply capability-specific enrichments using explicit capabilities.

required_engines.name: knowledge_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Add domain and stack best-practice guidance.

required_engines.name: architecture_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Generate or synthesize architecture from the canonical spec.

required_engines.name: story_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Generate implementation stories from the canonical spec.

required_engines.name: constraint_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Derive performance, scalability, security, and operational constraints.

required_engines.name: design_system_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Generate design-system and UX guidance where relevant.

required_engines.name: risk_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Identify risks from selected capabilities or project shape.

required_engines.name: validation
required_engines.responsibility: Validate structure and coverage of the generated project definition.

required_engines.name: refine_engine
required_engines.responsibility: Refine the final spec after synthesis.

understanding_requirements: Diagnosis must inspect both documentation and implementation.
Diagnosis must distinguish intended behavior from actual behavior.
Implementation files are the source of truth for current actual behavior.
Repository documents are the source of truth for intended direction and current iteration guardrails.

include: Understanding current behavior of the initializer
Semantic drift between archetype, capability, and feature
exclude: Production-hardening work outside current initializer concerns

success_criteria_for_diagnosis_iteration: The repository’s actual behavior is understood and recorded.
The intended conceptual model is documented.
The main structural inconsistencies are identified with evidence.
The main product idea remains unchanged.
The next implementation iteration has clear guardrails.
implementation_guardrails: Do not change product identity during diagnosis.
Do not use ambiguous field naming for canonical concepts.
Do not let downstream stages silently discard meaningful upstream state.
Do not merge archetype, capability, and feature into one field or one vocabulary.
Prefer explicit derivation rules and explicit merge behavior in future implementation.
minimum_working_definition: The initializer accepts a project description and CLI answers.
The initializer produces a valid spec object without runtime contract errors.
Archetype, capabilities, and features are represented consistently.
Architecture and stories are generated without destructive overwrite of required enriched state.
At least the editorial CMS flow runs end to end and writes coherent output artifacts.

stories.title: Map current CLI flow
stories.description: As an execution agent, I want to inspect and document the current CLI flow so that the team understands what inputs are collected and how the initializer currently starts its pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: The current CLI prompts are identified from implementation.
The order of prompt collection is documented.
The fields collected from the user are confirmed against code.
Any mismatch between documented intent and implemented CLI flow is recorded in diagnosis.md and progress.txt.

stories.title: Verify archetype detection contract
stories.description: As an execution agent, I want to verify what the archetype detector returns so that the repository can establish a stable canonical contract for archetype identity.
acceptance_criteria: The exact structure returned by detect_archetype is confirmed from code.
All consumers of spec.archetype are identified.
Any mismatch between expected and actual archetype fields is recorded.
The diagnosis clearly states whether archetype identity is currently stable or inconsistent.

stories.title: Verify capability production and application
stories.description: As an execution agent, I want to inspect how capabilities are produced and applied so that capability-driven behavior can be trusted in future iterations.
acceptance_criteria: The source of spec.capabilities is identified from code.
The capability registry keys are inspected.
The capability application path is confirmed from implementation.
Any mismatch between capabilities produced and capabilities consumed is recorded.

stories.title: Verify architecture synthesis flow
stories.description: As an execution agent, I want to inspect the architecture generation flow so that I can determine whether enriched architecture state is preserved or overwritten.
acceptance_criteria: The architecture generation order is confirmed from code.
Any upstream architecture enrichments are identified.
It is verified whether later stages merge or overwrite previous architecture data.
The result is documented in diagnosis.md and progress.txt.

stories.title: Verify story generation contract
stories.description: As an execution agent, I want to inspect how implementation stories are generated so that story identity, ordering, and coverage issues can be diagnosed safely.
acceptance_criteria: All story producers are identified.
Story ID generation strategy is confirmed from implementation.
Potential duplication or reset risks are recorded.
Any mismatch between capability-derived stories and generated stories is documented.

stories.title: Verify downstream engine alignment
stories.description: As an execution agent, I want to inspect downstream engines so that I can confirm whether constraints, risks, design, and diagram generation use canonical upstream names.
acceptance_criteria: Constraint, risk, design, and diagram engines are inspected.
Expected upstream field names are compared with actual producer output.
Any broken naming dependency is recorded.
The diagnosis explicitly lists downstream alignment problems.

stories.title: Define canonical model corrections
stories.description: As a technical lead, I want a clear implementation plan for canonical naming and pipeline contracts so that the next iteration can fix the repository without changing its main idea.
acceptance_criteria: The canonical roles of archetype, capability, feature, and answers are restated clearly.
The next implementation targets are ordered coherently.
The plan preserves the current product identity.
The plan is reflected in diagnosis.md, decisions.md, or prd.json as appropriate.

stories.title: Implement stable archetype identity
stories.description: As a product engineer, I want archetype detection to return a stable canonical identity so that all engines can consume spec.archetype consistently.
acceptance_criteria: The archetype detector returns a stable canonical identifier.
Consumers of spec.archetype are aligned with that contract.
The initializer runs without archetype key mismatch errors.
Validation performed is recorded in progress.txt.

stories.title: Implement explicit capability derivation
stories.description: As a product engineer, I want capabilities to be explicitly derived and stored in the spec so that capability handlers and downstream engines behave consistently.
acceptance_criteria: The spec always contains an explicit capabilities list when appropriate.
Capability application iterates canonical capability identifiers.
Capability registry keys match produced capabilities.
Validation performed is recorded in progress.txt.

stories.title: Preserve enriched architecture and story state
stories.description: As a product engineer, I want generators to preserve or intentionally merge upstream enriched state so that the pipeline behaves as a composition system rather than a destructive overwrite chain.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture enrichments are not silently discarded.
Story enrichments are not silently discarded.
A clear merge or composition strategy is implemented.
Validation performed is recorded in progress.txt.

stories.title: Align downstream engines with canonical names
stories.description: As a product engineer, I want downstream engines to consume the same canonical naming model used upstream so that derived outputs are complete and reliable.
acceptance_criteria: Constraint engine aligns with canonical upstream fields.
Design system engine aligns with canonical upstream fields.
Risk analysis aligns with canonical upstream fields.
Diagram generation aligns with canonical upstream fields.

stories.title: Validate editorial initializer flow end to end
stories.description: As a technical lead, I want an end-to-end validation using an editorial CMS prompt so that I can confirm the initializer now behaves coherently for its main reference scenario.
acceptance_criteria: An editorial-style prompt is executed through the initializer.
The generated spec is structurally coherent.
Architecture output is coherent with the selected archetype and capabilities.
Generated stories are present and non-contradictory.
The validation result is recorded in progress.txt.
